hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know about residency in Turkey. Get instant answers to common questions.

q1.question: What are the main types of residency permits in Turkey?
q1.intro: Turkey offers several residency options including:

q2.question: How long does the residency application process take?
q2.intro: Processing times vary by residency type:
q2.note: Our service typically expedites this within 10-15 business days.

q3.question: What documents do I need for residency application?
answers: Valid passport (minimum 6 months validity)
Proof of accommodation (rental contract or property deed)
Proof of income or financial means
Health insurance coverage
Criminal background certificate
Completed application form
q3.note: We provide a complete checklist customized to your specific situation.

q4.question: Can I work while on residency permit?
q4.intro: Work permissions depend on your residency type:

answers.label: Real Estate Residency
answers.text: Can establish business or become freelancer

answers.label: Work Visa
answers.text: Full employment rights with employer sponsorship

q4.note: We can guide you through obtaining work authorization alongside residency.

q5.question: What is the cost of getting Turkish residency?

answers.label: Service Fees
answers.text: Our professional assistance typically ranges €500-1,500 depending on complexity

q5.note: Get a free consultation for exact pricing based on your situation.

q1.question: Can I start a business in Turkey on residency?
q1.intro: Yes! Many residency types allow business ownership:
answers: Register as a sole proprietor or establish a limited company
Work visa holders can work for registered company
Real estate visa holders can own and manage businesses
Freelancers can operate as independent professionals
q1.note: We assist with company registration and work permits.

q2.question: What is required to sponsor an employee in Turkey?
q2.intro: As an employer, you'll need to:
answers: Have a registered Turkish company
Demonstrate financial stability
Provide employment contract
Pay mandatory social security contributions
Apply through Ministry of Labor and Social Security

q3.question: What are the taxation requirements for residents?
q3.intro: Taxation depends on your residency status:
answers: Residents can benefit from tax treaties if applicable
Annual tax filing required with Turkish authorities
q3.note: We recommend consulting with a tax professional for personalized advice.

q1.question: Is health insurance mandatory for residency?
answers: Turkish Social Security Insurance (SGK) is mandatory for working residents
Private insurance acceptable for non-working residents
Minimum coverage must include emergency care
Annual cost approximately ₺1,200-3,000
q1.note: We help arrange appropriate insurance coverage.

q2.question: Can I buy property as a foreign resident?
answers: Foreign nationals can purchase property with proof of funds
Property purchase can lead to residency qualification
Some regions have restrictions on land (agricultural areas)
Tax clearance required from country of origin
Total property value no more than 30% of Ankara value for security
q2.note: We connect you with trusted real estate partners.

q3.question: What happens if my residency expires?
answers: Most residencies are renewable annually
Apply 30-60 days before expiration
Requirements similar to initial application
Processing typically faster for renewals
Limited tourists can leave and re-enter for another 90 days
q3.note: We send renewal reminders and handle all paperwork.

q4.question: Can family members get residency based on my permit?
answers: Spouse and children can apply as dependents
Additional documentation proving relationship required
Financial support proof for dependents needed
Children gain automatic dependency rights
q4.note: Family reunion visas have special expedited processing.

q5.question: Is Turkish citizenship possible from residency?
q5.intro: Yes, citizenship is possible through residency:
answers: Continuous residency of 5+ years required
Language requirement (basic Turkish)
Character background check passed
Property investment of €250K+ qualifies for 3-year path
No renunciation of current nationality required
q5.note: Dual citizenship is permitted under Turkish law.
